title: hydrophylax carnosa
intro: Hydrophylax carnosa, also known as the Indian alder or water fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Hydrophylacaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. Hydrophylax carnosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat various health conditions.
